title: Te Whakatau Tātai o Te Awa: The Mathematics of River Restoration
subject: Mathematics
culturalContext: Students will investigate the restoration of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, the local river that holds deep significance for Ngāti Kahungunu. The river's name references the native fernbird (kōtukutuku) that once thrived along its banks. Using mathematical concepts, students will analyze historical flow data, calculate sediment loads, and design restoration strategies that honor both scientific principles and mātauranga Māori regarding water conservation and ecosystem balance.
learningObjectives: Calculate and compare river flow rates using volume and time measurements from historical and current data
Apply geometric principles to design effective riparian planting zones along the riverbank
Analyze statistical data on native bird populations to identify correlations with water quality improvements
Create scaled mathematical models proposing restoration solutions for specific sections of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku

activities.title: Te Horanga Wai - Understanding River Flow
activities.description: Students analyze historical flow data of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku using rate calculations and volume formulas. They compare pre-European settlement flow patterns with current data to understand environmental changes.

activities.title: Te Tātai Mata - Geometric Planting Designs
activities.description: Using aerial maps of the riverbank, students apply area and perimeter calculations to design native planting zones that prevent erosion while creating habitats for kōtukutuku (fernbird) and other native species.

activities.title: Ngā Tatauranga o Te Taiao - Statistical Analysis
activities.description: Students work with local conservation data to create graphs and charts showing relationships between water quality measurements and native species populations, calculating percentages and correlations.

activities.title: Te Whakaaturanga Whakamaori - Scale Modeling
activities.description: Using ratio and proportion, students create accurate scale models of proposed restoration sections, incorporating their mathematical calculations into physical representations of their solutions.

activities.title: Te Whakawhiti Kōrero - Community Presentation
activities.description: Students present their mathematical models and calculations to local kaumātua and environmental officers, explaining how their solutions balance mathematical precision with cultural and environmental values.

resources: Historical flow data from Hawke's Bay Regional Council for Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku
Ngāti Kahungunu kaumātua Rangiātea Whaanga for mātauranga Māori perspectives on water conservation
Topographic maps and aerial photographs of the river from Land Information New Zealand
Measuring equipment including flow meters, trundle wheels, and water testing kits from the school science department

tasks: Complete mathematical calculations workbook showing all formulas and methods used
Create a scaled restoration plan with accurate measurements and ratios
Present findings to community stakeholders using appropriate mathematical language and visual representations
Write a reflection connecting mathematical concepts to cultural values of kaitiakitanga

nzcAlignment: Number and Algebra: Patterns and relationships, Equations and expressions
Geometry and Measurement: Measurement, Position and orientation
Key Competencies: Using language, symbols, and texts; Participating and contributing
